participant_backends.local_collective_realization: CUDA ranks realize the shared gradient contract through the generic dense CUDA runtime and an NCCL-compatible fp32 all-reduce surface.
participant_backends.master_weight_role: Each CUDA rank holds a mirrored fp32 master-weight view and may only apply the optimizer step after the shared fp32 gradient barrier.

participant_backends.local_collective_realization: MLX ranks canonicalize local gradients into the shared fp32 bridge format instead of claiming native NCCL participation or backend-local quantized exchange.
participant_backends.master_weight_role: Each MLX rank mirrors the same fp32 master-weight state and may only apply the optimizer step after the shared fp32 gradient barrier.

collective_contract.bridge_detail: The first mixed CUDA-plus-MLX dense mesh uses one canonical fp32 gradient all-reduce plus fp32 master-weight broadcast contract. Backend-local kernels may differ, but the shared wire contract, step barrier, and math surface stay identical.

optimizer_ownership.master_weight_authority: Mirrored fp32 master weights on every rank with one shared post-all-reduce step barrier.
optimizer_ownership.optimizer_state_authority: Mirrored fp32 AdamW state on every rank until the later mixed-backend checkpoint contract lands.
optimizer_ownership.step_commit_posture: Every rank applies the same optimizer step only after the shared fp32 gradient barrier and the canonical step hash agree.
optimizer_ownership.detail: The first mixed CUDA-plus-MLX dense mesh stays on the simplest honest optimizer law: mirrored fp32 AdamW state and mirrored fp32 master weights on every rank. Later sharded or checkpoint-portable optimizer ownership remains a separate issue.

proof_preconditions: #539 first same-job CUDA-plus-MLX dense proof run

refusal_set.detail: The first shared CUDA-plus-MLX dense mesh refuses BF16 mixed precision and keeps one fp32 reference law so backend-local numerical differences do not leak into the shared step contract.
The first shared CUDA-plus-MLX dense mesh refuses fp16 loss scaling because MLX and CUDA must first agree on one typed overflow and underflow contract before low-precision scaling is admissible.
The shared mixed-backend mesh refuses to pretend an MLX Metal rank can participate in NCCL directly. MLX gradients must first canonicalize into the shared fp32 bridge format.
The shared mixed-backend mesh refuses split master-weight authority. CUDA and MLX ranks must carry the same mirrored fp32 master weights and the same post-barrier step hash.
The shared mixed-backend mesh refuses checkpointless optimizer-state migration. Backend transitions must go through the later typed mixed-backend checkpoint family.

claim_boundary: This contract closes one typed CUDA-plus-MLX dense mesh math law: fp32 gradient all-reduce, fp32 master-weight broadcast, mirrored fp32 AdamW state, and one explicit refusal set for unsupported mixed-backend operations. It does not claim checkpoint portability or a real proof run by itself.
